item.description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

media:content.media:title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’
media:content.media:description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Dan De Luce
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon is increasingly concerned about Israel ramping up its spying on the U.S., recently raising the counterintelligence threat level from America’s top ally in the Middle East to the highest level, according to two U.S. officials and one former U.S. official

media:content.media:text: Trump Mideast Wars Gaza
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump Israeli and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu speak after Netanyahu addressed the Knesset, Israel's parliament, Monday, Oct. 13, 2025, in Jerusalem.

item.title: Pentagon raised threat of Israeli spying on U.S. to highest level, sources say

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Israel faces mounting international outrage over allegations of rape, beatings and humiliation of international activists detained on a flotilla attempting to break its naval blockade of Gaza.

media:content.media:text: A black eye can be seen on Suza's face
media:content.media:description: A man named Suza, one of the Americans who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla to Gaza, shows a bruise on his eye he said he received while in Israeli captivity, after returning to John F. Kennedy Airport, in New York City, on May 24, 2026.

media:content.media:text: People raise their fists as they walk past a crowd carrying Palestinian flags
media:content.media:description: Activist Saif Abukeshek, center, and fellow flotilla participants arrive at El Prat Airport, in Catalonia, Spain, on May 23.

media:content.media:text: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.
media:content.media:description: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.

media:content.media:text: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.

item.title: Israel faces growing pressure over alleged abuse of Gaza flotilla members

item.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Beccera will advance to the general election in the race for governor in California, NBC News projects. NBC News’ Liz Kreutz discuss the latest election results and what impacts the speed of vote counting in the state.

media:content.media:title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to California governor general election, NBC News projects
media:content.media:description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Beccera will advance to the general election in the race for governor in California, NBC News projects. NBC News’ Liz Kreutz discuss the latest election results and what impacts the speed of vote counting in the state.

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to California governor general election, NBC News projects

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
item.description: Hundreds of supporters signaled they weren’t phased by the new allegations about Graham Platner’s past relationships, giving the Democratic Senate candidate a warm welcome on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, speaks at a campaign event Friday, June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine.

item.title: ‘Maine had my back’: Graham Platner hits the campaign trail amid latest turmoil

item.description: Two former NYPD detectives speak exclusively with Lester Holt about those frantic days after the murder of the United Healthcare CEO in broad daylight in Midtown Manhattan. They describe how they ultimately identified him in a Dateline NBC special airing tonight.

media:content.media:title: Former NYPD detectives speak out about Luigi Mangione manhunt for first time
media:content.media:description: Two former NYPD detectives speak exclusively with Lester Holt about those frantic days after the murder of the United Healthcare CEO in broad daylight in Midtown Manhattan. They describe how they ultimately identified him in a Dateline NBC special airing tonight.

item.title: Former NYPD detectives speak out about Luigi Mangione manhunt for first time

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Joe Murphy
item.description: The House issues a rare rebuke of the president, FIFA imposes a ban, and a flesh-eating parasite returns.
item.title: A ‘60 Minutes’ veteran gets fired, and a rookie hits an unusual home run: The news quiz

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Sebastian Berhalter’s path to the World Cup is not that of a young prodigy who had an easy way onto the U.S. men’s national team — despite what some might think of the son of storied former player and head coach Gregg Berhalter

media:content.media:text: Sebastian Berhalter dribbling a soccer ball during a game.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

media:content.media:text: Gregg Berhalter standing on the sidelines of a soccer field.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

item.title: Sebastian Berhalter turned his father’s tough truth into World Cup breakthrough

item.dc:creator: Joe Kottke
item.description: A nonprofit and five Utah residents have filed a lawsuit against officials and a special entity overseeing Kevin O’Leary’s planned Stratos data center.

media:content.media:text: Consensus Miami 2026
media:content.media:description: Kevin O'Leary in Miami Beach, Flo., last month.

item.title: Utah residents sue officials over Kevin O’Leary data center plan

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Israel faces mounting international outrage over allegations of rape, beatings and humiliation of international activists detained on a flotilla attempting to break its naval blockade of Gaza.

media:content.media:text: A black eye can be seen on Suza's face
media:content.media:description: A man named Suza, one of the Americans who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla to Gaza, shows a bruise on his eye he said he received while in Israeli captivity, after returning to John F. Kennedy Airport, in New York City, on May 24, 2026.

media:content.media:text: People raise their fists as they walk past a crowd carrying Palestinian flags
media:content.media:description: Activist Saif Abukeshek, center, and fellow flotilla participants arrive at El Prat Airport, in Catalonia, Spain, on May 23.

media:content.media:text: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.
media:content.media:description: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.

media:content.media:text: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.

item.title: Israel faces growing pressure over alleged abuse of Gaza flotilla members

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Sebastian Berhalter’s path to the World Cup is not that of a young prodigy who had an easy way onto the U.S. men’s national team — despite what some might think of the son of storied former player and head coach Gregg Berhalter

media:content.media:text: Sebastian Berhalter dribbling a soccer ball during a game.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

media:content.media:text: Gregg Berhalter standing on the sidelines of a soccer field.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

item.title: Sebastian Berhalter turned his father’s tough truth into World Cup breakthrough

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Joe Murphy
item.description: The House issues a rare rebuke of the president, FIFA imposes a ban, and a flesh-eating parasite returns.
item.title: A ‘60 Minutes’ veteran gets fired, and a rookie hits an unusual home run: The news quiz

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
Scott Wong
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is seeking to put his stamp on the city’s congressional delegation with a trio of endorsements against incumbent lawmakers and their picks, a move that could further expand the power of his democratic socialist movement or thwart its progress — and dent his own image in the process.“This is high-risk, high reward,” said Rebecca Katz, the prominent Democratic strategist and ally whose firm worked with Mamdani during his 2025 run.

media:content.media:text: Zohran Mamdani, left, and Brad Lander speak into press microphones outside
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

media:content.media:text: Darializa Avila Chevalier smiles for a portrait
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

media:content.media:text: Claire Valdez speaks outside in front of people holding signs
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

item.title: Mamdani makes ‘high-risk, high-reward’ move to reshape New York’s voice in Washington

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
item.description: Hundreds of supporters signaled they weren’t phased by the new allegations about Graham Platner’s past relationships, giving the Democratic Senate candidate a warm welcome on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, speaks at a campaign event Friday, June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine.

item.title: ‘Maine had my back’: Graham Platner hits the campaign trail amid latest turmoil

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: The zoo this week launched an online petition against the data center that, as of Friday, had more than 180,000 signatures and 25,000 shares on Facebook.

media:content.media:text: Sumatran tiger cubs
media:content.media:description: Sumatran tiger cubs explore the tiger exhibit at the Nashville Zoo in Nashville, Tenn.

item.title: Nashville Zoo tries to halt data center project next door over animal safety concerns

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Dan De Luce
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon is increasingly concerned about Israel ramping up its spying on the U.S., recently raising the counterintelligence threat level from America’s top ally in the Middle East to the highest level, according to two U.S. officials and one former U.S. official

media:content.media:text: Trump Mideast Wars Gaza
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump Israeli and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu speak after Netanyahu addressed the Knesset, Israel's parliament, Monday, Oct. 13, 2025, in Jerusalem.

item.title: Pentagon raised threat of Israeli spying on U.S. to highest level, sources say

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
item.description: BAR HARBOR, Maine — Rep. Ro Khanna of California on Friday said that Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner should apologize to the women who recently came forward describing disturbing behavior they experienced with him

media:content.media:text: Ro Khanna.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-CA) arrives for a House Oversight Committee closed-door interview with former prison guard Tova Noel on Capitol Hill, last month.

item.title: Graham Platner supporter Ro Khanna says he should apologize to women who alleged disturbing behavior

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: A National Park Service mountain guide died when she fell into a crevasse on Mount McKinley, officials said Friday
item.title: National Park mountain guide dies in fall on Mount McKinley

item.dc:creator: Liz Kreutz
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: A federal prosecutor indicated the investigations are focused on the Los Angeles area following President Donald Trump’s claims, without evidence, of cheating in the primaries.

media:content.media:text: Voters cast their ballots
media:content.media:description: Voters cast their ballots at a polling location inside Metro Headquarters during a primary election in Los Angeles on Tuesday.

item.title: DOJ office says ‘multiple’ probes of California elections underway after Trump cries foul

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Trump administration is urging European countries to mirror U.S. travel restrictions implemented in response to Ebola as the U.S. prepares to host the largest-ever World Cup amid growing concerns about the spread of the disease, according to a State Department cable reviewed by NBC News

media:content.media:text: Health workers wearing protective equipment walk through the isolation area of a newly established Ebola treatment center at the Evangelical Medical Center in the Rwampara Health Zone on May 31, 2026 in Bunia, Democratic Republic of Congo.
media:content.media:description: Health workers in an Ebola treatment center in Bunia, Democratic Republic of the Congo, on May 31.

item.title: U.S. warns Europe to step up Ebola screening ahead of World Cup

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has advanced to the general election in the California governor’s race, NBC News projects

media:content.media:text: Xavier Becerra
media:content.media:description: California gubernatorial candidate Xavier Becerra delivers remarks during the California Democratic Party convention at Moscone Center in San Francisco on Saturday, February 21, 2026.

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to general election in California governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Natasha Korecki
item.description: Some of Maine Gov. Janet Mills’ most ardent supporters are encouraging her to reactivate her suspended Senate campaign as fellow Democrat Graham Platner faces new allegations of “toxic” behavior toward three ex-girlfriends, which he has denied

media:content.media:text: Janet Mills
media:content.media:description: Janet Mills in Westbrook, Maine, on April 17. She dropped out of the Senate race weeks later.

item.title: Janet Mills shows no sign of reigniting her Maine Senate bid amid Graham Platner’s troubles

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
Andrew Blankstein
item.description: A man has been charged with murder in the death of actor James Handy, who was found stabbed outside his girlfriend’s Los Angeles home this week, the district attorney’s office said Friday

media:content.media:text: James Handy
media:content.media:description: "Vishy-Vashy-Vinnie" - Airdate: January 17, 1995.

item.title: Son of James Handy’s girlfriend is charged with murder in actor’s stabbing death

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: A federal judge last week temporarily blocked the $1.8 billion fund from being used after a Jan. 6 prosecutor sued to block the Trump administration’s actions.

media:content.media:text: DOJ Oversight House
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche during a hearing in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: DOJ urges judge not to block $1.8B fund that it says is already dead
